title: AGENTS.md
text: Provide reliable Windows workflow using gdalraster self-contained GDAL runtime built CI. Primary user flow: Auto-bootstrap flow (runtime source-built gdalraster already installed): Also support explicit runtime activation:
code: pak::pak("jimbrig/gdalraster.windows") gdalraster.windows::install_gdal_runtime() gdalraster.windows::install_gdalraster() gdalraster.windows::load_gdalraster() gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names() library(gdalraster.windows) library(gdalraster) gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names() gdalraster.windows::load_gdal_dll() library(gdalraster) gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names()

title: AGENTS.md
text: Build modern GDAL (currently 3.13+) source Windows CI using MSYS2 UCRT64 / Rtools45-compatible MinGW toolchain. Build required features project, including muparser, keep runtime self-contained. top-level GDAL runtime DLL (libgdal-*.dll) required non-Windows dependent DLLs required runtime data directories (share/gdal, share/proj) pure-python GDAL utilities (python/osgeo_utils) embedded-python algorithms (e.g. gdal driver gpkg validate) Build gdalraster source bundled GDAL runtime. Ensure runtime loading configured gdalraster resolves bundled DLL dependencies runtime (without relying matching user-installed Rtools environment), GDAL’s embedded python can import bundled osgeo_utils (session-scoped PYTHONPATH). Verify success gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names() returning non-empty output driver gpkg validate algorithm running end--end. runtime installs package-managed user data paths source builds target isolated library path unless explicitly overridden

title: AGENTS.md
text: .github/workflows/build.yml tools/build_gdal.sh tools/collect_dlls.sh

title: AGENTS.md
text: use modern package-style R explicit namespacing. use cli user-facing messaging cli::cli_abort() errors. use rlang::caller_env() rlang::caller_arg() validators/errors. use withr scoped state (with_makevars, env vars, lib paths); avoid leaking persistent session/user config. default source-install target gdalraster must non-destructive (isolated library path), overwrite existing user/global installs unless explicitly requested. keep docs implementation-anchored general; avoid session-specific claims.

title: Using gdalraster.windows on Windows
text: GDAL algorithms (e.g. gdal driver gpkg validate) implemented Python: libgdal embeds CPython interpreter runtime imports pure-python osgeo_utils package. runtime bundle ships package <gdal_home>/python, activate_gdal_runtime() exposes embedded interpreter prepending PYTHONPATH (session-scoped). python.exe must discoverable PATH GDAL embed interpreter (Rtools’ UCRT64 python works, system python). algorithm fails ModuleNotFoundError: module named 'osgeo_utils', runtime bundle predates python support — reinstall install_gdal_runtime(overwrite = TRUE) re-activate.
code: library(gdalraster.windows) library(gdalraster)  alg <- gdalraster::gdal_alg(cmd = "driver gpkg validate") alg$setArg("dataset", "path/to/file.gpkg") alg$setArg("full-check", TRUE) alg$run() alg$output() #> [1] "Checking gpkg_spatial_ref_sys\n...\nValidation succeeded"

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: repository started practical Windows failures gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names() empty toolchain states. now provides maintained runtime path isolated default.

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: section technical rationale current design.

what: 1) Root problem: static registration + MXE/static builds
title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: GDAL’s Algorithm API uses static C++ registration. Windows build states (notably specific Rtools/MXE combinations), top-level algorithm registry observed load return names. practice means gdalraster::gdal_global_reg_names() can return character(0) even GDAL present. project solves controlling GDAL build profile runtime loading path, rebuilding gdalraster known runtime.

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: MinGW-w64: GCC-based Windows compiler toolchain native .exe/.dll. MSYS2: package manager + shell environment used assemble toolchains dependencies (pacman). UCRT64: MSYS2 toolchain target using Microsoft’s Universal CRT. Rtools45: Windows R package build toolchain, also UCRT-based. practical requirement keep compile/runtime toolchains compatible (MinGW/UCRT alignment) avoid C/C++ runtime ABI mismatch problems.

what: 3) Static vs dynamic linking in this repository
title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: runtime pieces linked statically (portability GCC runtime bits). GDAL delivered shared runtime DLL (libgdal-*.dll) transitive dependencies bundled alongside . words: project “single-file static GDAL”, self-contained runtime bundle controlled dependency closure.

what: 4) C++ ABI compatibility (why source rebuild is required)
title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: gdalraster binds GDAL C++ APIs via compiled code. Even headers match, ABI mismatches can break runtime binaries built incompatible compiler/runtime combinations. package installs gdalraster source bundled GDAL headers/import libs rather assuming arbitrary prebuilt binary compatible.

what: 5) Key CMake/linker flags used and why
title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: Current build uses flags tools/build_gdal.sh, including: -DGDAL_USE_MUPARSER=muparser-enabled algorithm functionality -DGDAL_HIDE_INTERNAL_SYMBOLS=reduce export-surface pressure Windows -Wl,--kill-Windows/MinGW export naming behavior -static-libgcc -static-libstdc++ static winpthread handling reduce external runtime fragility flags practical stability choices observed Windows build/runtime behavior, arbitrary tuning.

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: Building libgdal-*.dll sufficient . Runtime success depends non-Windows transitive DLL dependencies present. tools/collect_dlls.sh performs recursive dependency inspection (ntldd -R), copies required UCRT64 DLLs bundle, fails unresolved external non-Windows dependencies remain.

what: 7) Windows DLL load order and preloading
title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: Compile-time link success guarantee runtime load success. Windows still needs resolve libgdal-*.dll dependency tree active process. activate_gdal_runtime() addresses : prepending bundle bin/ PATH setting GDAL/PROJ data env vars prepending bundle python/ PYTHONPATH (see section 9) optionally preloading libgdal-*.dll dyn.load(..., local = FALSE, now = TRUE) explicit runtime activation exists addition source builds.

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: GDAL PROJ require runtime data files (share/gdal, share/proj). Without , CRS related functionality can fail even DLL loading succeeds. activate_gdal_runtime() sets: GDAL_DATA PROJ_LIB PROJ_DATA installed bundle available.

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: GDAL CLI algorithms implemented Python rather C++. example, gdal driver gpkg validate thin C++ entry point libgdal embeds CPython interpreter runtime: GDAL locates python.exe PATH, dynamically loads matching libpython DLL, calls Py_Initialize(), imports osgeo_utils.samples.validate_gpkg. osgeo_utils pure-Python package shipped GDAL’s gdal-utils distribution (swig/python/gdal-utils/ GDAL source tree). contains compiled extension modules, CPython ABI coupling — embedded interpreter version can import . runtime bundle ships package python/osgeo_utils, version-locked built GDAL tag. activate_gdal_runtime() prepends <gdal_home>/python PYTHONPATH (session-scoped; never persisted user machine environment) embedded interpreter can resolve . PYTHONPATH read Py_Initialize(), libgdal triggers lazily first use embedded-python algorithm, activation-time configuration early enough. Without , algorithms fail : Note: compiled osgeo SWIG bindings (osgeo import gdal) intentionally built bundled (BUILD_PYTHON_BINDINGS stays — Python/SWIG build environment). pin bundle single CPython version/ABI. Python-implemented validators degrade gracefully without (e.g. validate_gpkg skips tiled gridded coverage checks).
code: GDAL FAILURE 1: ... ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'osgeo_utils'

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: separate concerns: Compile-time: headers/libs via Makevars (PKG_CPPFLAGS, PKG_LIBS) Runtime: DLL resolution via process environment loader state package scopes compile-time settings install calls (withr), manages runtime activation separately session reliability.

title: Windows GDAL Runtime and gdalraster Bootstrap Tools
text: users want persistence, add_gdal_rprofile_hook() writes managed startup block can load runtime context early session. optional design; default behavior stays non-destructive local.

title: Add or update an .Rprofile hook for bundled GDAL — add_gdal_rprofile_hook
text: rprofile Target .Rprofile path. gdal_home GDAL home directory. lib Library path containing custom gdalraster install. dry_run TRUE, return updated file contents without writing.

title: Add or update an .Rprofile hook for bundled GDAL — add_gdal_rprofile_hook

title: Install precompiled GDAL runtime — install_gdal_runtime
code: install_gdal_runtime(   repo = "jimbrig/gdalraster.windows",   tag = "latest",   asset_pattern = "gdal-(bundle|ucrt64)-.*\\.zip$",   gdal_home = default_gdal_home(),   overwrite = FALSE,   local_zip = NULL,   fallback_zip = NULL )

title: Install precompiled GDAL runtime — install_gdal_runtime
text: repo GitHub repo slug, e.g. "jimbrig/gdalraster.windows". tag Release tag "latest". asset_pattern Regex used select release asset. gdal_home Destination GDAL home directory. overwrite Whether replace existing gdal_home. local_zip Optional local GDAL runtime zip install directly. fallback_zip Optional fallback zip path used release download fails. NULL (default), vendored zip inst/extdata/gdal-ucrt64-fallback.zip used present; none ships package, default fallback attempted.

title: Install precompiled GDAL runtime — install_gdal_runtime
text: Invisibly returns installed GDAL home path.

title: Install precompiled GDAL runtime — install_gdal_runtime
text: local_zip (highest precedence), GitHub release asset lookup/download, fallback_zip release lookup/download fails. selected zip must contain GDAL root bin/libgdal-*.dll.

title: Install precompiled GDAL runtime — install_gdal_runtime
text: machines without network access, download release asset manually https://github.com/jimbrig/gdalraster.windows/releases, transfer target machine, install directly:   Note fallback zip shipped package (full runtime bundle large vendor), fallback path applies provide fallback_zip .

text: lib.loc Optional library location used loading gdalraster. activate_runtime Whether run activate_gdal_runtime() first. gdal_home GDAL home used activate_runtime = TRUE. quiet TRUE, suppress sitrep CLI output.

title: Verify gdalraster algorithm API availability — verify_gdalraster_runtime
text: TRUE algorithm API available, otherwise FALSE.
